task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: Explanation and use of the Nozzle Classification chart

abstract.p: The Nozzle Classification Chart is printed when the operator selects Automatic nozzle optimization under Tools, Automatic Maintenance or Manual Maintenance. (duration 9 minutes)
The Nozzle Classification chart is scanned by the M&C unit in order to determine misdirected and non-jetting nozzles.
If these failing nozzles are not detected by PAINt, they are permanently switched off until the moment you redo this test.
Depending on the print mode the nozzle failure compensation mechanism will try to compensate these missing nozzles with good working nozzles. The mechanism is completely automated.
In the CMYKW configuration (white ink hardware installed), the Printhead Quality check chart also contains a gradient for the white ink next to the CMYK color.

note.p: The chart is not to be used for a visual check!

section.title: Detailed information
section.p: Depending on the configuration, the nozzle classification chart includes white or not. In the example below the white hardware is included.
1 - Scanner x resolution calibration to enable side shooter detection in x-direction.
2,3,4,5 - Patterns to get the nozzles in good condition. No visual conclusions possible.
1 chip-chip transition visible. This might be visible and is not resulting to PQ artifacts.

li.p: example of a white line in a test pattern
single nozzle droplets which are measures by the scanner to conclude the status Misdirected or Non-jetting.

section.title: Use of the test print for service
section.p: In case you suspect a printhead is out of specification the Nozzle Classification chart is used to determine which printhead has the problem combined with the input from SDS test 04-6-002 Get nozzle status.
